name: Mining Engineering
description: Mining Engineering covers the science and technology of extracting minerals from the earth safely and efficiently. The curriculum includes surface and underground mining, rock mechanics, mine ventilation, mineral processing, and mine safety.

subjects.name: Mining Geology
subjects.description: Study of geological principles relevant to mining including mineralogy, petrology, structural geology, and ore deposit formation. Covers geological mapping, exploration techniques, and resource estimation.
learning_outcomes: Identify minerals and rocks relevant to mining operations
Interpret geological maps and cross-sections
Apply exploration techniques for mineral deposit discovery
Estimate mineral resources and reserves
subjects.industry_relevance: Geological knowledge is fundamental to mineral exploration, mine planning, and resource estimation in the mining industry.

subjects.name: Surface Mining
subjects.description: Study of surface mining methods including open-pit, open-cast, and strip mining. Covers mine planning, drilling and blasting, loading and hauling, and reclamation of mined lands.
learning_outcomes: Design open-pit and open-cast mining layouts
Plan drilling and blasting operations for surface mines
Select loading and hauling equipment for surface operations
Develop mine reclamation and closure plans
subjects.industry_relevance: Surface mining accounts for the majority of mineral production worldwide, driving demand for skilled mining engineers.

subjects.name: Underground Mining
subjects.description: Study of underground mining methods including room-and-pillar, longwall, cut-and-fill, and sublevel stoping. Covers mine development, ground support, ore handling, and underground mine planning.
learning_outcomes: Select appropriate underground mining methods for different deposits
Design mine development and excavation sequences
Implement ground support systems for underground openings
Plan ore handling and transportation systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Underground mining is essential for accessing deep mineral deposits, with growing emphasis on safety and automation.

subjects.name: Rock Mechanics
subjects.description: Study of mechanical behavior of rocks including stress-strain analysis, failure criteria, rock mass classification, and slope stability. Covers in-situ stress measurement, rock testing, and support design.
learning_outcomes: Analyze stress distribution around underground excavations
Classify rock masses using standard systems
Design rock support systems for tunnels and stopes
Evaluate slope stability in open-pit mines
subjects.industry_relevance: Rock mechanics is critical for safe and efficient mine design, tunnel construction, and civil engineering projects.

subjects.name: Mine Ventilation
subjects.description: Principles of mine ventilation including airflow analysis, fan selection, ventilation network design, and environmental control in underground mines. Covers gas detection, dust control, and thermal environment management.
learning_outcomes: Design ventilation systems for underground mines
Analyze airflow networks using ventilation simulation software
Select and position fans for optimal air distribution
Monitor and control mine environmental parameters
subjects.industry_relevance: Mine ventilation is critical for worker safety, health, and productivity in underground mining operations.

subjects.name: Mine Safety Engineering
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of safety engineering in mining operations including hazard identification, risk assessment, accident prevention, and emergency response. Covers mining legislation, safety standards, and disaster management.
learning_outcomes: Conduct risk assessments for mining operations
Design safety management systems for mines
Investigate mining accidents and implement preventive measures
Apply mining safety legislation and standards
subjects.industry_relevance: Mine safety is paramount, with strict regulations and growing adoption of technology for hazard prevention and monitoring.

subjects.name: Mine Surveying
subjects.description: Surveying techniques specific to mining operations including surface and underground surveys, tunnel alignment, volume calculations, and deformation monitoring. Covers total stations, GPS, and laser scanning.
learning_outcomes: Conduct surface and underground mine surveys
Use total stations and GPS for mine measurement
Calculate volumes of excavated materials
Monitor ground deformation using surveying techniques
subjects.industry_relevance: Mine surveying is essential for accurate mine planning, excavation control, and regulatory compliance.

subjects.name: Blasting Engineering
subjects.description: Science and technology of rock blasting including explosives classification, blast design, initiation systems, vibration monitoring, and fragmentation analysis. Covers safety, environmental impact, and regulatory aspects.
learning_outcomes: Design blast patterns for surface and underground operations
Select appropriate explosives and initiation systems
Monitor blast vibrations and control environmental impacts
Analyze fragmentation results and optimize blast designs
subjects.industry_relevance: Blasting is the primary rock fragmentation method in mining, requiring specialized expertise for safe and efficient operations.

subjects.name: Mineral Processing
subjects.description: Study of mineral beneficiation processes including crushing, grinding, screening, classification, froth flotation, magnetic separation, and gravity concentration. Covers process flowsheet design and plant operation.
learning_outcomes: Design mineral beneficiation flowsheets
Operate crushing, grinding, and classification equipment
Apply froth flotation and gravity separation techniques
Optimize mineral recovery and grade in processing plants
subjects.industry_relevance: Mineral processing converts raw ore into marketable concentrates, essential for the economics of mining operations.

subjects.name: Mine Environment Engineering
subjects.description: Environmental aspects of mining including air and water pollution, noise control, land degradation, and ecosystem restoration. Covers environmental impact assessment, mine closure planning, and sustainable mining practices.
learning_outcomes: Conduct environmental impact assessments for mining projects
Design pollution control systems for mining operations
Develop mine closure and reclamation plans
Implement sustainable mining practices and environmental monitoring
subjects.industry_relevance: Environmental compliance is mandatory for mining operations, with increasing focus on sustainability and ESG standards.

subjects.name: Mining Machinery
subjects.description: Study of mining machinery including drills, loaders, dump trucks, excavators, continuous miners, and longwall shearers. Covers machine selection, maintenance, and automation of mining equipment.
learning_outcomes: Select mining machinery for specific operations and conditions
Design maintenance schedules for mining equipment
Evaluate machine performance and productivity metrics
Implement automation and teleoperation of mining machinery
subjects.industry_relevance: Mining machinery represents a major capital investment, with automation and electrification transforming equipment design.

subjects.name: Mine Planning
subjects.description: Strategic and tactical mine planning including reserve estimation, production scheduling, equipment selection, and economic analysis. Covers mine design software, pit optimization, and life-of-mine planning.
learning_outcomes: Develop life-of-mine plans for surface and underground operations
Optimize pit limits using economic analysis
Schedule production to meet grade and tonnage targets
Use mine planning software for design and scheduling
subjects.industry_relevance: Mine planning determines the economic viability and operational efficiency of mining projects over their lifespan.

subjects.name: Opencast Mining
subjects.description: Specialized study of opencast mining methods including bench design, dragline operations, shovel-dumper combinations, and in-pit crushing. Covers highwall stability, dewatering, and production optimization.
learning_outcomes: Design bench geometry and slope angles for opencast mines
Plan dragline and shovel-dumper operations
Implement dewatering systems for opencast mines
Optimize production sequencing in opencast operations
subjects.industry_relevance: Opencast mining is the most common method for coal and metalliferous mining, requiring specialized planning expertise.

subjects.name: Tunnel Engineering
subjects.description: Design and construction of tunnels for mining, transportation, and civil engineering applications. Covers tunneling methods, ground support, tunnel boring machines, and ventilation during construction.
learning_outcomes: Design tunnel layouts and excavation sequences
Select appropriate tunneling methods for ground conditions
Design ground support systems for tunnels
Plan tunnel ventilation and safety during construction
subjects.industry_relevance: Tunnel engineering is critical for mining access, transportation infrastructure, and underground space utilization.

subjects.name: Coal Mining
subjects.description: Specialized study of coal mining methods including bord-and-pillar, longwall, and opencast coal mining. Covers coal seam characteristics, methane drainage, spontaneous heating, and coal beneficiation.
learning_outcomes: Design bord-and-pillar and longwall mining layouts for coal
Implement methane drainage and ventilation systems in coal mines
Manage spontaneous heating and fire hazards in coal mines
Design coal beneficiation plants for quality improvement
subjects.industry_relevance: Coal remains a major energy source globally, with ongoing demand for efficient and safe coal mining technologies.

subjects.name: Mining Legislation
subjects.description: Study of mining laws, regulations, and policies including Mines Act, Mineral Concession Rules, environmental regulations, and safety statutes. Covers mine inspection, legal compliance, and statutory reporting.
learning_outcomes: Interpret mining legislation and regulatory requirements
Ensure compliance with safety and environmental statutes
Prepare statutory reports and documentation for mines
Understand legal aspects of mineral rights and concessions
subjects.industry_relevance: Mining legislation knowledge is mandatory for mine managers and essential for legal compliance in mining operations.
